info.title: MeshQu API
info.description: Governance infrastructure for policy-aware AI decisions. MeshQu does not run tools. It governs decisions about them.

apiKey.description: MeshQu API key passed as a bearer token: `Authorization: Bearer mqu_…`. Mint one in the console (Settings → API keys).

tenantId.name: X-MeshQu-Tenant-Id
tenantId.description: Tenant UUID for multi-tenant isolation. Required on all authenticated routes — validated before authentication (middleware/tenant.ts), so a missing or non-UUID header returns 400 (MISSING_TENANT_ID / INVALID_TENANT_ID) before the API key is checked.

metricsToken.description: Prometheus scrape token (`METRICS_BEARER_TOKEN`) for `GET /metrics`. Distinct from the API key; only required when the token is configured.

get.description: Returns service health status. Does not check dependencies.
Returns service readiness status. Checks database, RLS enforcement, and Redis connectivity.

error.description: Error message if Redis check failed

active_role.description: Current database role after SET LOCAL ROLE

error.description: Error message if RLS check failed
Error message if Redis check failed

active_role.description: Current database role after SET LOCAL ROLE

error.description: Error message if RLS check failed

get.description: Returns paginated list of policies for the tenant.

shadow_mode.description: When true, violations are logged but DENY becomes ALERT

policy_group_id.description: Policy group this policy belongs to

regulatory_refs_warnings.description: Warnings for regulatory_refs entries not found in the clause registry. Informational only — the save proceeded.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

versionState.description: Lifecycle state of the selected version. "submitted" is reserved for PV2-020; today the server only emits active | draft | historical.

permissions.description: Convenience permission flags. Clients MUST use actions[] as the source of truth for availability; permissions are for disable-state/tooltip hints only.

items.description: Header action. actions[] is server-computed; the client renders one button per entry in order. Unavailable actions are OMITTED, not disabled.

post.description: Creates a new policy with initial version.

decision_type.description: Decision type this policy applies to (e.g., "lc_approval")

rules.description: Policy rules for initial version (can be empty, add rules via version endpoint)

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

shadow_mode.description: When true, violations are logged but DENY becomes ALERT. Enables A/B testing.

policy_group_id.description: Optional policy group to assign this policy to

shadow_mode.description: When true, violations are logged but DENY becomes ALERT

policy_group_id.description: Policy group this policy belongs to

regulatory_refs_warnings.description: Warnings for regulatory_refs entries not found in the clause registry. Informational only — the save proceeded.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

versionState.description: Lifecycle state of the selected version. "submitted" is reserved for PV2-020; today the server only emits active | draft | historical.

permissions.description: Convenience permission flags. Clients MUST use actions[] as the source of truth for availability; permissions are for disable-state/tooltip hints only.

items.description: Header action. actions[] is server-computed; the client renders one button per entry in order. Unavailable actions are OMITTED, not disabled.

post.description: Validates policy rules without saving. Use this to check rules before creating a policy.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

get.description: Returns a policy with its current version plus PV2 editor context (versionState, selectedVersion, permissions, actions).

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Which version to surface. **Default since 2026-05-17: "latest"** — the version the editor should open: draft if one exists (in-flight work), else the active ratified, else the highest version number. Only 404 if the policy has no versions at all. "active" — the ratified+active version, 404 if none exists (use this for compliance/audit consumers that must distinguish "ratified" from "merely exists"). "draft" — the first draft, 404 if none exists. Numeric — that exact version, 404 if missing.

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

get.description: Returns all versions of a policy.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

post.description: Promotes a draft version to ratified status, making it the active version governing evaluations. Returns a signed PolicyApprovalReceipt when a ratifier key is configured.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

policy_approval_receipt.description: APR-005: Signed approval receipt produced at ratification time. Null when the ratifier key is not configured.

kid.description: Key ID of the ratifier signing key.

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over the canonical pre-sig payload.

post.description: Transitions a draft policy version to "submitted" state. Requires the actor to be the draft author. Blocks if another draft or submitted version exists for the same policy. The request body is intentionally omitted from the schema so callers may invoke this endpoint with no body (Fastify rejects `undefined` bodies when a body schema is attached).

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

post.description: Transitions a submitted policy version back to draft with a required human-readable reason. Blocks when actor == maker and maker-checker enforcement is enabled.

reason.description: Required human-readable rejection reason. Must contain at least one non-whitespace character. Surfaced in the audit timeline and the draft banner.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

post.description: Transitions a submitted policy version back to "draft" state. The request is intentionally bodyless (recall has no payload). Requires the actor to be the version's original submitter — foreign callers receive 403 FORBIDDEN_NOT_SUBMITTER.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

get.description: Cursor-paginated decision log for a single policy. Supports rule / type / date / version filters. If ?version is omitted, defaults to the active version — matching the "this version" default in the console Evidence tab.

parameters.name: rule
parameters.description: Filter to decisions whose violations include this rule code

parameters.name: version
parameters.description: Filter to decisions recorded under this policy version (defaults to the active version when omitted — matches the "this version" UI default)

parameters.name: cursor
parameters.description: Opaque cursor returned by the previous page. Omit for the first page.

200.description: Cursor-paginated decision log scoped to a policy

schema.description: Cursor-paginated decision log scoped to a policy

items.description: A recorded decision with full context

source_artifact.description: Cryptographic binding to a source document

type.description: Type of source artifact

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the artifact (64 lowercase hex characters)

reference_id.description: Client reference ID for the artifact

byte_size.description: Size of the artifact in bytes

decision.description: Final decision: ALLOW, DENY, REVIEW, or ALERT

rule_code.description: Unique code identifying the rule that was violated

reason_code.description: Structured reason code for programmatic aggregation

actual_value.description: Actual value that caused the violation

policy_id.description: Policy ID that owns the violated rule

is_shadow.description: Whether this violation came from a shadow-mode policy

rules_na.description: Number of rules skipped due to when clause not matching

na_rules.description: Rules that were not applicable due to when clause

when.description: The when clause that excluded the rule

reason.description: Human-readable reason why the rule was not applicable

policy_snapshot_id.description: UUID of the frozen policy snapshot used

evaluated_rules_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the evaluated rules (code, condition, severity, when)

integrity_hash.description: SHA-256 integrity hash of the entire evaluation

signature.description: Base64url-encoded Ed25519 signature over the integrity hash

signature_kid.description: Key ID of the signing key used

transparency_anchor.description: Sigstore Rekor transparency anchor, null if anchoring failed

anyOf.description: Direct URL to the Rekor entry

action.description: Action bound to the receipt (null when not bound)

anyOf.description: Action bound to this decision receipt

type.description: Type of action authorized (e.g., payment_execute, loan_approve)

reference_id.description: Client's reference ID for the action (e.g., INV-88423)

receipt_schema_version.description: Receipt envelope schema version (1 = legacy, 2 = RCP2)

anyOf.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical policy snapshot
SHA-256 hex digest of the evidence manifest (if provided)

shadow_converted.description: Whether shadow mode converted this decision (DENY suppressed to ALERT because every violation came from a shadow-mode policy). Bound into the integrity hash.

original_decision.description: Decision before shadow-mode conversion (null when no conversion occurred). Bound into the integrity hash.

chain_id.description: Chain ID if this decision is part of a decision chain

parent_decision_id.description: Parent decision within the same chain

chain_integrity_hash.description: SHA-256 chain integrity hash (Phase 2 cryptographic chain proof)

chain_signature.description: Chain proof signature. Post-DAH-205 records carry "chain-proof/v1:" + base64url(Ed25519 over the domain-separated chain-proof/v1 envelope); legacy records carry base64url(Ed25519 over the bare chain_integrity_hash).

chain_signature_kid.description: Key ID that produced the chain signature

policy.description: Policy metadata from the snapshot used for evaluation

governance_source.description: Attribution of which tenant policies governed this decision

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID whose policies governed this decision

tenant_name.description: Human-readable tenant name (snapshot at evaluation time)

level.description: Governance level: 0 = local tenant, 1+ = inherited from parent

policy_group_id.description: Policy group ID for filtering and audit

policy_group_code.description: Policy group code for filtering and audit

evaluated_policies.description: Policies evaluated in this decision (populated for group evaluations)

items.description: A policy that was evaluated as part of a decision

version.description: Policy version at time of evaluation

shadow_mode.description: Whether policy was in shadow mode

rules.description: Rules that were evaluated within this policy

items.description: A rule within an evaluated policy

actor.description: Actor attribution — who or what made this decision

type.description: Whether the actor is human or automated

next_cursor.description: Opaque cursor for the next page, or null on the final page

patch.summary: Update draft version rules in place
patch.description: Autosave-style mutation of an in-flight draft version. Replaces the rules JSONB on the existing draft row — does NOT create a new version and does NOT write an audit entry. Returns 409 if the target version is not a draft; returns 404 if the version does not exist for the caller's tenant.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)
Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

delete.description: Hard-deletes an in-flight draft version. Returns 409 if the version is not a draft (submitted requires recall first); 404 if not found for the caller's tenant.

get.description: Returns a specific version of a policy.

replaces_rule_code.description: Advisory lineage: rule code this replaces (not included in snapshot hash)

status.description: Lifecycle status: draft → submitted → ratified → superseded (or back to draft on reject)

get.description: Returns paginated list of policy groups for the tenant.

policy_count.description: Number of policies in this group

post.description: Creates a new policy group.

get.description: Returns a policy group by ID.

delete.description: Deactivates a policy group (soft delete). Cannot delete groups with active policies.

get.description: Returns paginated list of policies belonging to a policy group.

post.description:
Evaluates policies against a decision context without persisting.

Returns:
- **decision**: ALLOW, DENY, REVIEW, or ALERT
- **violations**: Rule violations with details
- **integrity_hash**: Cryptographic proof of evaluation
- **snapshot**: Policy state used

Use POST /v1/decisions/record to evaluate AND persist.

source_artifact.description: Cryptographic binding to a source document

type.description: Type of source artifact

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the artifact (64 lowercase hex characters)

reference_id.description: Client reference ID for the artifact

byte_size.description: Size of the artifact in bytes

action.description: Action bound to this decision receipt

type.description: Type of action authorized (e.g., payment_execute, loan_approve)

reference_id.description: Client's reference ID for the action (e.g., INV-88423)

requestBody.description: Request to evaluate policies against a decision context

rule_code.description: Unique code identifying the rule that was violated

reason_code.description: Structured reason code for programmatic aggregation

actual_value.description: Actual value that caused the violation

na_rules.description: Rules that were not applicable due to when clause

when.description: The when clause that excluded the rule

reason.description: Human-readable reason why the rule was not applicable

evaluated_rules_hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the evaluated rules (code, condition, severity, when)

integrity_hash.description: SHA-256 integrity hash of the entire evaluation

signature_kid.description: Key ID of the signing key used

transparency_anchor.description: Sigstore Rekor transparency anchor, null if anchoring failed

anyOf.description: Direct URL to the Rekor entry

action.description: Action bound to the receipt (null when not bound)

anyOf.description: Action bound to this decision receipt

type.description: Type of action authorized (e.g., payment_execute, loan_approve)

reference_id.description: Client's reference ID for the action (e.g., INV-88423)

receipt_schema_version.description: Receipt envelope schema version (1 = legacy, 2 = RCP2)

anyOf.description: SHA-256 hex digest of the canonical policy snapshot
SHA-256 hex digest of the evidence manifest (if provided)

shadow_converted.description: Whether shadow mode converted this decision (DENY suppressed to ALERT because every violation came from a shadow-mode policy). Bound into the integrity hash.

original_decision.description: Decision before shadow-mode conversion (null when no conversion occurred). Bound into the integrity hash.

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the snapshot content

rules_count.description: Number of rules in the snapshot

policies_count.description: Number of policies in the snapshot

cached.description: Whether the snapshot was reused from cache

timing.description: Per-phase timing breakdown for the evaluation

snapshot_resolve_ms.description: Time to resolve policy snapshot in milliseconds

engine_evaluate_ms.description: Time for pure policy engine evaluation in milliseconds

source.description: Source of the policy snapshot: cache hit or database lookup

enabled.description: True if policy is in shadow mode (observe but not enforce)

original_decision.description: Original decision before shadow mode conversion (only present if converted)

governance_source.description: Attribution of which tenant policies governed this decision

tenant_id.description: Tenant ID whose policies governed this decision

tenant_name.description: Human-readable tenant name (snapshot at evaluation time)

level.description: Governance level: 0 = local tenant, 1+ = inherited from parent

post.description:
Evaluates policies AND persists the result with full audit trail.

Supports idempotency: if the same idempotency_key is provided twice,
returns the original decision without re-evaluating.

Returns:
- **decision**: The recorded decision with full context
- **is_new**: False if returned from idempotency cache
- **duration_ms**: Total request duration

schema.description: Request to record a decision with evaluation

source_artifact.description: Cryptographic binding to a source document

type.description: Type of source artifact

hash.description: SHA-256 hash of the artifact (64 lowercase hex characters)

reference_id.description: Client reference ID for the artifact

byte_size.description: Size of the artifact in bytes

idempotency_key.description: Unique key to prevent duplicate recordings

chain.description: Decision chain linkage for workflow/case grouping

chain_id.description: Chain ID to group this decision into an ordered sequence

chain_step.description: Step number within the chain (auto-assigned if omitted)

parent_decision_id.description: Parent decision within the same chain (causal lineage)

action.description: Action bound to this decision receipt

type.description: Type of action authorized (e.g., payment_execute, loan_approve)

reference_id.description: Client's reference ID for the action (e.g., INV-88423)

actor.description: Actor attribution — who or what made this decision

type.description: Whether the actor is human or automated

evidence_manifest_digest.description: Evidence manifest digest — null or absent means no evidence is bound

anyOf.description: SHA-256 hex digest (64 lowercase hex chars) of the canonical evidence manifest

evidence_manifest.description: Full evidence manifest object — server stores and indexes it

anyOf.description: Multi-item evidence manifest bound to a governed decision

manifest_version.description: Manifest schema version. Currently locked to "1".

items.description: Evidence items examined for this decision
One artifact examined when making a governed decision

kind.description: Category of this evidence item (e.g. kyc_record, sanctions_screening_result). Named EvidenceItemKind values have UI labels; unknown strings are displayed verbatim.

mime_type.description: IANA media type of the artifact (caller-attested)

byte_length.description: Size of the artifact in bytes (caller-attested)

source_system.description: Identifier of the system that produced or holds the artifact

artifact_locator.description: Optional caller-attested pointer to the immutable, version-pinned artifact (additive v2.x)

uri.description: Resolvable address of the artifact within the custodian environment

version_id.description: Immutable version pin; MAY be omitted only when uri is content-addressed

retrieval_time.description: ISO 8601 timestamp at which the artifact was retrieved from the source system

custodian_id.description: Identifier of the custodian that provided or attests to this artifact

normalization_profile.description: Identifier of the normalisation profile applied before hashing, if any

content_hash.description: SHA-256 content hash of the artifact (64 lowercase hex chars)

signature.description: Optional Ed25519 custodian signature over this item

anyOf.description: Custodian signature over a single evidence item

custodian_kid.description: Key ID of the custodian signing key (opaque string; convention: <custodian_id>/<label>)

signature.description: Ed25519 signature over sha256(canonical(item minus this field)), base64url-encoded

algorithm.description: Signing algorithm. Fixed to ed25519 in manifest_version 1.

manifest_digest.description: Self-describing manifest digest (optional; computed by server if absent)

anyOf.description: Pre-computed sha256(canonical(manifest minus this field)). Server re-computes and validates when present; computes when absent.

Expected for REVIEW decisions. Optional for DENY/ALERT. Rare for ALLOW.
One outcome per decision — subsequent attempts return 409.

post.description:
Re-evaluates a recorded decision using the same snapshot and timestamp.
Used for audit verification - if matches=true, the decision is authentic.

Returns:
- **original**: The original recorded decision
- **replay**: The replayed evaluation result
- **matches**: True if integrity hashes match
- **duration_ms**: Total replay duration

signature_verification.description: Signature verification status for the original receipt during replay

post.description: Seals a chain, proving completeness. No more steps can be added after sealing. Returns 422 (CHAIN_SEAL_NOT_TERMINAL) if the last step is a held REVIEW decision rather than a terminal disposition (ALLOW/DENY/ALERT).

get.description: Returns the seal for a chain, or 404 if not sealed.

post.description:
Verifies all decisions in a chain. For each step:
- Recomputes the integrity hash and compares to the stored hash
- Checks for gaps in the step sequence (reported as warnings)
- Validates parent linkage (parent exists in chain with lower step)

Phase 1 verification is database-trust: individual receipts are
cryptographically verified, chain structure relies on DB constraints.

200.description: Result of verifying all decisions in a chain

schema.description: Result of verifying all decisions in a chain

valid.description: True if all individual receipts pass integrity verification

chain_sealed.description: Whether the chain has been sealed

seal.description: Seal verification result (null if not sealed)

get.description: Returns all webhook subscriptions for the tenant.

post.description:
Creates a webhook subscription for alert notifications.

**Webhook URL requirements (SSRF protection):**
- Must be HTTPS in production.
- The host must resolve to a **publicly-routable** address. URLs that resolve
  to private, loopback, link-local, cloud-metadata (`169.254.169.254`),
  CGNAT, or otherwise reserved ranges are rejected — at create time **and**
  re-checked at delivery time. A subscription whose host later resolves to a
  non-public address stops delivering and is dead-lettered with the reason on
  the delivery record.
- Internal-service ports (SSH, Postgres, Redis, etc.) are blocked.

If a legitimate endpoint is being rejected, an operator can add its
`host` or `host:port` to the `WEBHOOK_SSRF_ALLOWLIST` server config. This
is operator-only configuration and is never self-service.

**IMPORTANT**: The response includes a `secret` field that is shown **only once**.
Save this secret securely - it cannot be retrieved again.

Webhooks are delivered with:
- X-MeshQu-Event: alert.created
- X-MeshQu-Alert-Id: uuid
- X-MeshQu-Tenant-Id: uuid
- X-MeshQu-Timestamp: Unix milliseconds
- X-MeshQu-Signature: sha256=<hmac>

Subscriptions filter alerts by:
- decision_types: Array of decision types to subscribe to
- severity_min: Minimum severity level (alerts with equal or higher severity are delivered)

**HMAC Signature Verification:**
All webhooks are signed. To verify:
1. Extract X-MeshQu-Timestamp and X-MeshQu-Signature headers
2. Compute: HMAC-SHA256(secret, timestamp + "." + body)
3. Compare: "sha256=" + hex(result) === X-MeshQu-Signature
4. Reject if timestamp is older than 5 minutes (replay protection)

secret.description: Webhook secret for HMAC-SHA256 signature verification. IMPORTANT: Save this securely - it will NOT be shown again.

get.description:
Returns paginated list of webhook deliveries for the tenant.

Use this to monitor webhook delivery status and debug failed deliveries.

**Status values:**
- `pending`: Awaiting delivery or retry
- `delivered`: Successfully delivered
- `failed`: Failed, retry scheduled
- `dead`: Permanently failed after max attempts
Returns aggregate statistics for webhook deliveries.
Returns details of a specific webhook delivery.

post.description:
Manually retry a failed or dead-lettered webhook delivery.

Resets the attempt count and schedules immediate retry.
Only works for deliveries in `failed` or `dead` status.

get.description: Returns paginated list of alerts for the tenant.
Returns a specific alert.

post.description: Marks an alert as acknowledged.

get.description: Returns all API keys for the tenant. Never includes the actual key value.

post.description: Creates a new API key. The plaintext key is only returned once - store it securely!

scopes.description: Permission scopes. Defaults to read + evaluate. The operator-level tenants:provision scope is not grantable here (granted out-of-band only).

plaintext_key.description: Full API key - only shown once. Store securely! Format: mqu_<43 chars base64url>

get.description: Returns an API key by ID. Never includes the actual key value.

delete.description: Revokes an API key. Revoked keys cannot be used for authentication.

get.description: Returns paginated list of audit events for the tenant with optional filters.
Returns all audit events for a specific entity in chronological order.

post.description: Verifies the integrity of the audit log hash chain. Optionally scoped by date range.

Returns activity metrics for a specific policy including decision volume, outcomes, daily trends, and top violations. Pass ?version=<n> to filter aggregates to decisions recorded under a specific policy version.

parameters.description: Filter aggregates to decisions recorded under a specific policy version. Omit for the legacy cross-version aggregate.

get.description: Returns field names for autocomplete suggestions, optionally filtered by prefix.
Returns the tenant settings object.

patch.description: Merges the provided settings into the existing tenant settings.

post.description: Appends a rule creation feedback entry for the data flywheel and audit trail.

get.description: Public keys used for receipt signature verification (current + previous during rotation).

post.description: Creates a new attested manual check form derived from a policy.

policy_id.description: Policy to derive the form from

scope.description: Evaluation scope: policy = linked policy only, decision_type = all policies for the decision type

password.description: Password for password mode (will be hashed)

token_expires_at.description: ISO 8601 expiry for token-mode access (defaults to 7 days from creation)

token_max_uses.description: Maximum number of submissions allowed via token

token_secret.description: Token secret — only returned on create/regenerate (one-time reveal)

get.description: Returns the signed receipt for a form submission. Self-verifying.

chain_id.description: Chain ID if this decision is part of a chain

get.description: Returns the signed receipt for any recorded decision. Self-verifying — contains the integrity hash, signature, and transparency anchor.

chain_id.description: Chain ID if this decision is part of a chain

get.description: Returns a Verification Bundle v1 archive — a self-describing, digest-addressed JSON envelope containing the receipt, the frozen policy snapshot, the trusted-keys snapshot at export time, and a manifest whose digest covers them all. The bundle lets an external verifier validate the receipt offline. Public and unauthenticated.
Returns a Verification Bundle for every decision in a chain, ordered by chain_step. Each step is a complete, independently verifiable receipt bundle. Public and unauthenticated.
Verifies all decisions in a chain: per-step integrity hash recomputation, chain linkage proof, step continuity, and seal verification if sealed. Public and unauthenticated.

200.description: Result of verifying all decisions in a chain

schema.description: Result of verifying all decisions in a chain

valid.description: True if all individual receipts pass integrity verification

chain_sealed.description: Whether the chain has been sealed

seal.description: Seal verification result (null if not sealed)

200.description: Public seal proof for a decision chain

schema.description: Public seal proof for a decision chain

patch.description: Sets or clears the per-tenant default-lens override. Pass null to clear (fall back to computed default). Returns the new resolver state. An explicit override is honoured by the resolver even when it disagrees with the computed result.

post.summary: Activate a regulation on this tenant
post.description: Records tenant intent to surface the given regulation on the dashboard. Writes one row to meshqu.tenant_regulations and one audit_log entry. Does NOT create, scaffold, or tag any policies — tagging policies with regulatory_refs is a separate step (services-led during pilot onboarding in v1).

delete.summary: Deactivate a regulation on this tenant
delete.description: Flips is_active to false and stamps deactivated_at on the tenant_regulations row. Idempotent — deactivating an already-deactivated regulation returns 200 without error. Writes one audit_log entry. Does NOT delete the row so activation history stays intact.

get.description: Returns clause metadata plus per-card counts + samples. Composite id format {regulation_code}-{clause_id}. 404 on unknown clause; 403 when the clause's regulation is not activated on the tenant. Samples match RecordedDecision shape.

parameters.name: id
parameters.description: Composite clause id in the form {regulation_code}-{clause_id}

items.description: RecordedDecision-shaped sample row (structurally a DecisionRecord superset).

no_policies_tagged.description: True when no tenant policies are tagged with this clause id (broken-ref state).

warnings.description: Per-card warnings. Empty when the card is healthy. When `no_policies_tagged` is true, carries one entry of type `no_policies_tagged` with plan §S.11 copy.

get.summary: List every registered clause (tenant-independent registry read)
get.description: Returns every composite clause id the regulation registry knows about, with its regulation context. Tenant-independent — activation state is not consulted. Consumed by the console clause picker (DASH-018).

composite_id.description: Composite clause id {regulation_code}-{clause_id} — the tag string stored in meshqu.policies.regulatory_refs.

get.description: Returns policy-group metadata plus the four standard card payloads (DASH-019) in canonical order. 404 when the group does not exist on the caller's tenant — this includes groups that exist on OTHER tenants (collapsed to 404 to avoid leaking existence). Samples match RecordedDecision shape.

items.description: RecordedDecision-shaped sample row (structurally a DecisionRecord superset).

get.description: Returns the tier-1 dashboard payload for the requested lens. If `lens` is omitted, resolves to the tenant default per DASH-021. Regulation lens has both an active branch and a zero-state branch; policy-group lens always returns the active branch with groups rendered (zero-count cards if the tenant has no decisions). Policy-group responses never include `has_broken_references` or `warning_banner` — those are regulation-lens-only.

200.description: Dual-lens overview payload. See the route docblock for the full per-branch shape.

schema.description: Dual-lens overview payload. See the route docblock for the full per-branch shape.
